name: Basal Metabolic Rate Calculator (Industrial Engineering Standard)
description: Calculates Basal Metabolic Rate using the Mifflin-St Jeor equation with industrial-grade adjustments for energy efficiency, lean body mass estimation, and metabolic confidence scoring. Compliant with ISO 8996 (ergonomics of the thermal environment) and Lean Six Sigma measurement system analysis.

inputs.label: Gender
inputs.businessContext: Required for Mifflin-St Jeor equation coefficient selection.

inputs.label: Body Weight
inputs.businessContext: Total body mass measured on calibrated scale (ISO 9001:2015 calibration traceability).

inputs.label: Height
inputs.businessContext: Stature measured per ISO 7250-1:2017 basic human body measurements.

inputs.label: Age
inputs.businessContext: Chronological age used in metabolic rate decay modeling.

inputs.label: Body Fat Percentage (optional)
inputs.businessContext: Used for lean body mass estimation (DEXA or skinfold per ISAK standards). If null, default estimation is applied.

inputs.label: Activity Factor (PAL)
inputs.businessContext: Physical Activity Level per WHO/FAO/UNU expert consultation (2004). Used for total energy expenditure estimation.

inputs.label: Measurement Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Subjective assessment of input data quality (e.g., calibrated equipment, self-reported vs. lab). Influences dataConfidenceAdjusted output.

rules.message: BMI less than 12 is physiologically improbable. Please verify weight and height.
BMI greater than 60 may indicate data entry error. Please verify inputs.
Age below 18 is outside standard BMR equation validation range.
For non-sedentary activity levels, body fat percentage improves lean body mass estimation accuracy.

thresholds.message: BMR below 1000 kcal/day may indicate extreme underweight or measurement error.
BMR above 3000 kcal/day is rare; verify inputs for possible outliers.
Lean body mass below 30 kg may indicate sarcopenia or data error.

formulas.name: Mifflin-St Jeor BMR
formulas.description: Basal metabolic rate using the Mifflin-St Jeor equation (1990), the most validated equation for the general population.
formulas.equation: IF gender = 'male' THEN (10 * weight_kg) + (6.25 * height_cm) - (5 * age_years) + 5 ELSE (10 * weight_kg) + (6.25 * height_cm) - (5 * age_years) - 161

formulas.name: Lean Body Mass (LBM)
formulas.description: Estimated lean body mass using Boer formula (1984) if body_fat_percent is not provided, else direct calculation.
formulas.equation: IF body_fat_percent IS NOT NULL THEN weight_kg * (1 - body_fat_percent/100) ELSE IF gender = 'male' THEN (0.407 * weight_kg) + (0.267 * height_cm) - 19.2 ELSE (0.252 * weight_kg) + (0.473 * height_cm) - 48.3

formulas.name: BMR Adjusted for Lean Mass
formulas.description: Adjusts Mifflin-St Jeor BMR using lean body mass factor (industrial energy efficiency correction).

formulas.name: Total Energy Expenditure (TEE)
formulas.description: Estimated total daily energy expenditure using PAL factor (WHO/FAO/UNU 2004).
formulas.equation: bmr_adjusted_for_lean_mass * CASE activity_factor WHEN 'sedentary' THEN 1.2 WHEN 'lightly active' THEN 1.375 WHEN 'moderately active' THEN 1.55 WHEN 'very active' THEN 1.725 WHEN 'extra active' THEN 1.9 END

formulas.name: Metabolic Efficiency Index (MEI)
formulas.description: Ratio of BMR to lean body mass, normalized to population average (31 kcal/kg LBM/day). Indicates metabolic efficiency per unit of metabolically active tissue.

formulas.name: Data Confidence Factor
formulas.description: Multiplier based on measurement_confidence input to adjust final BMR for data quality (ISO 5725 accuracy trueness and precision).
formulas.equation: CASE measurement_confidence WHEN 'low' THEN 0.85 WHEN 'medium' THEN 1.0 WHEN 'high' THEN 1.05 END

formulas.name: Primary BMR Result (Data Confidence Adjusted)
formulas.description: Final BMR value adjusted for lean mass and data confidence. This is the primary output.

primary.label: Basal Metabolic Rate (Adjusted)
primary.description: Final BMR value incorporating lean mass adjustment and data confidence factor.

bmr_mifflin_st_jeor.label: Mifflin-St Jeor BMR
bmr_mifflin_st_jeor.description: Unadjusted BMR from the standard equation.

bmr_adjusted_for_lean_mass.label: BMR (Lean Mass Adjusted)
bmr_adjusted_for_lean_mass.description: BMR after lean mass efficiency correction.

total_energy_expenditure.label: Total Energy Expenditure
total_energy_expenditure.description: Estimated daily energy needs including activity.

metabolic_efficiency_index.label: Metabolic Efficiency Index
metabolic_efficiency_index.description: Metabolic rate per kg of lean body mass.

data_confidence_factor.label: Data Confidence Factor
data_confidence_factor.description: Multiplier applied based on measurement confidence.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Missing Body Fat Data
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Body fat percentage not provided; lean mass estimated via Boer formula, which may introduce up to ±5% error in LBM.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Use DEXA or skinfold measurements per ISAK protocol.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Activity Factor Uncertainty
hiddenLossDrivers.description: PAL values are population averages; individual variation can cause ±20% error in TEE.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Use accelerometry or heart rate monitoring for personalized PAL.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Measurement Confidence
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Self-reported or uncalibrated measurements reduce accuracy. Data confidence factor of 0.85 applied.
hiddenLossDrivers.mitigation: Use calibrated equipment and trained personnel (ISO 10012 measurement management).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Age-Related Metabolic Decay
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Standard equations assume linear decay; actual metabolic rate may vary due to muscle mass loss not captured by age alone.

suggestedActions.label: Improve Body Fat Measurement
suggestedActions.description: Use DEXA or BIA with validated equations to reduce LBM estimation error.
suggestedActions.expectedImprovement: Reduction in BMR uncertainty by up to 3%.

suggestedActions.label: Calibrate Weight Scale
suggestedActions.description: Ensure scale is calibrated per ISO 9001:2015 to reduce weight measurement error.
suggestedActions.expectedImprovement: Reduction in weight error from ±0.5 kg to ±0.1 kg.

suggestedActions.label: Use Activity Monitor
suggestedActions.description: Replace PAL estimation with 7-day accelerometry for accurate TEE.

suggestedActions.label: Repeat Measurements for Gage R&R
suggestedActions.description: Perform repeated measurements to assess measurement system variation (ANOVA Gage R&R per AIAG MSA 4th ed.).
suggestedActions.expectedImprovement: Quantify and reduce measurement system variation.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: The primary BMR result already incorporates data confidence adjustment via the data_confidence_factor.
